human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for beverage-manufacturing specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, traceability and release-boundary handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to beverage execution, syrup or blend readiness, batch and lot evidence, blocked-work escalation, and completion evidence.
Authority sources cover FDA and eCFR beverage-manufacturing baselines, product-family-specific juice and bottled-water rules, GS1 traceability standards, and SQFI beverage operating references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, engineering and supplier boundary rejection, traceability conflicts, quality holds, unsupported completion, conflicting-policy escalation, execution exceptions, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-and-beverage-manufacturing/beverage-operations-specialist/ so beverage operations remain distinct from adjacent quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, logistics, and food-safety lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final quality release, engineering change approval, supplier approval, recall authority, and safety override authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated beverage operations specialist for syrup and blend readiness, beverage production execution, blocked-work handling, lot traceability, quality-boundary escalation, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
